stories.title: Starving soldiers’ scandal rocks Ukrainian army
stories.summary: KYIV — Ukraine’s frontline soldiers are starved and forced to drink rainwater, according to reports. The army’s General Staff removed an army commander and demoted another after pictures of emaciated infantrymen appeared online. The soldiers served in the 14th brigade, and the two officers responsible for the men were accused of not reporting the situation to higher command. Soldiers of the second battalion stationed in the Kharkiv region, on the battlefront across the Oskil River, w

stories.title: Greta Thunberg, Sally Rooney and Brian Eno defy Palestine Action ban in letter to judges
stories.summary: Exclusive: Scholars, writers and artists risk arrest with message of support for proscribed group before next week’s appeal hearing UK politics live – latest updates Sally Rooney, Greta Thunberg and Brian Eno have written to the court of appeal in support of Palestine Action before next week’s hearing to determine the lawfulness of the ban on the direct action protest group. The letter, composed of only seven words – “We oppose genocide, we support Palestine Action” – is signed by more than 130

stories.title: The Guardian view on Anthropic’s Claude Mythos: when AI finds every flaw, who controls the internet? | Editorial
stories.summary: Tech can scale cyber-attacks and defences alike, raising questions about private power, public risk and the future of a shared internet Anthropic announced its latest AI model, Claude Mythos , this month but said it would not be released publicly, because it turns computers into crime scenes. The company claimed that it could find previously unknown “zero-day” flaws, exploit them and, in principle, link these weaknesses in order to take over major operating systems and web browsers . Mythos did

stories.title: Justice department watchdog will investigate release of Epstein files – US politics live
stories.summary: An internal watchdog will review the agency’s handling of records related to Jeffrey Epstein, including whether relevant documents were properly redacted Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox When Pete Hegseth was asked about Pope Leo XIV’s condemnation of the war in Iran , and comments from the US Conference of Catholic Bishops suggesting the conflict is not a “just war”, the defense secretary simply said that the pope was “going to do his thing” . “We kn

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strike on Odesa kills 2, hits hospitals and schools overnight
stories.summary: Ukraine said Russian forces struck the Black Sea port city of Odesa overnight on 23 to 24 April 2026, killing at least two people and injuring more than a dozen.

stories.title: Tusk celebrates ‘no Russians in the room’ at EU summit — then winks at Fico
stories.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk told reporters there is “huge relief” among leaders at Friday’s EU summit in Cyprus because, “for the first time in years, there are no Russians in the room” — before winking at journalists as his Kremlin-friendly Slovak counterpart Robert Fico strolled past. “No, no, it’s a joke,” Tusk quickly added. Fico has been one of the bloc’s most vocal dissenters on Ukraine policy — second only to the obstructionist outgoing Hungarian leader Viktor Orbán, who

stories.title: Europe is in a profound state of crisis. Luckily, we know what to do | Nathalie Tocci and Anu Bradford
stories.summary: We assembled a group of the continent’s leading thinkers to assess the threats: their warnings are stark, but the remedy is within reach Caught between Vladimir Putin’s Russia, Donald Trump’s US and Xi Jinping’s China, Europe appears in a state of profound crisis, the narrative about its future often filled with fatalism. There is a paradox, however. Despite rising nationalism, the climate crisis and the economic slowdown , few would take issue with the claim that Europe still has a great deal g

stories.title: At first summit without Orbán, Zelenskyy rejects “symbolic” EU membership
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU approves €90 billion loan for Ukraine after Hungary lifts controversial veto.

stories.title: Woman killed by bear in Polish forest, son and local government say
stories.summary: In an extremely rare fatal encounter, the 58-year-old was killed as she was walking in a mountainous area, her son told rescuers.

stories.title: Donald Tusk szerint türelmesnek kell lenni Magyar Péterrel, de ő is okozhat csalódást egyeseknek
stories.summary: A lengyel miniszterelnök szerint hiába várja az EU-s baloldal, hogy Magyar Péter progresszív lesz.

stories.title: Co Lee és a 33 dalos Air 2: új korszak indul a Budapest Parkban (x)
stories.summary: Április 20-án debütál Co Lee és don sea közös nagylemeze, az Air 2, amely összesen 26 trackkel és egy különleges vinyl-kiadáson 7 bónuszdallal feszegeti a hazai hiphop határait. A WAVY Records gondozásában megjelent anyag alkotói folyamata egyfajta belső meditációként, lassan hömpölygő folyóként érkezik a hallgatókhoz. A valaha volt legnagyobb volumenű magyar nyelvű lo-fi hiphop anyag négyévnyi munka gyümölcseként ölt testet.

stories.title: Prosecutor’s office suddenly eager to investigate suspicious cases in Hungary, which speaks volumes about fallen regime
stories.summary: Immediately after Fidesz's crushing defeat, the prosecutor’s office took action in two cases in which virtually nothing had happened up to that point. A look at why this may be, and what could still be expected in light of the Tisza Party's promise of calling to account.

stories.title: Márciusban visszafordult az ijesztő trend a magyar munkanélküliségben
stories.summary: Egy hirtelen megugrás után ismét mérséklődött az adat, de azért még mindig nem rózsásak a várakozások a munkaerőpiacon.

stories.title: Fico helyettese szerint a Beneš-dekrétumokkal a szlovák állam csak visszaveszi, ami az övé
stories.summary: A parlamentben is téma volt a földek kérdése, amibe a szlovák kormány most a korábbiaknál jobban beleáll.

stories.title: Hungarians have been using these lands illegally since 1945 – Slovak deputy PM says
stories.summary: Robert Kaliňák spoke about one of the most sensitive issues affecting future Hungarian-Slovak relations: the Beneš Decrees.

stories.title: “He held us hostage for a long time”—Orbán’s EU colleagues bid him farewell at EU summit he skipped
stories.summary: According to the Belgian PM, there is a bit too much euphoria over the Hungarian PM's departure, and he suggested that it might not necessarily be easy to agree on everything with Péter Magyar either.

stories.title: A HÖR Budapesten: berlini klubhangulat élőben a KASSA Hajón (x)
stories.summary: A berlini klubkultúra egyik legismertebb online platformja, a HÖR most szombaton Budapestre érkezik. A KASSA Hajó és a The Etiket közös szervezésében megvalósuló esemény egy olyan nemzetközi formátumot hoz el a városba, amely az elmúlt években a globális elektronikus zenei közeg egyik meghatározó referenciapontjává vált.

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid: Most Budapestért is így tudom a legtöbbet tenni
stories.summary: A leendő közlekedési és beruházási miniszter sok feladatot sorolt, prioritás lesz a vasút, de a KRESZ megújításával, a kátyúmentesítéssel és a közösségi közlekedéssel is lesz tennivaló.

stories.title: Karácsony Gergely szívből gratulált Vitézy Dávidnak a miniszteri jelöléshez
stories.summary: A főpolgármester szerint nagyon kellett már egy Budapest fejlesztéséért elkötelezett miniszter.

stories.title: A magyar történelemben először lesz miniszter egy látássérült ember
stories.summary: Kátai-Németh Vilmos ügyvéd és aikidomester lesz a szociális és családügyi miniszter, aki korábban önvédelmi rendszert fejlesztett vakoknak, a kampányban pedig még egy vonatot is lefutott a Gubacsi hídon.

stories.title: Török Gábor: Az alakuló Magyar-kormány leginkább egy Fidesz light
stories.summary: Az elemző abból vezeti le ezt, hogy a kormánytagok egy része komoly beosztásban dolgozott az Orbán-kormányok idején. De szerinte ez nem klasszikus pártpolitikai kormány lesz.

stories.title: Ez lesz az 5 legsürgetőbb feladata Vitézy Dávidnak, az új közlekedési miniszternek
stories.summary: Nemcsak a vasút helyzetének rendbehozatala lesz sürgető, amiről Magyar Péter is sokat beszélt. Az új miniszternek egyebek mellett dolga lesz Mészáros Lőrinc és Szíj László hitbizományaival is.

stories.title: Lannert Juditot jelöli Magyar Péter oktatási miniszternek
stories.summary: A közgazdász és szociálpolitikus végzettséggel, szociológiából PhD fokozattal rendelkező Lannert Judit 1990 óta foglalkozik oktatáskutatással. Szerinte türelemre, időre, levegőre van szükség az oktatásban.

stories.title: Nemrég még hársfateázni küldte az őt kiosztó Lázár Jánost, most beülhet a székébe
stories.summary: Vitézy Dávid már tizenévesen rendszeresen nyilatkozott közlekedési témákban a sajtóban, aztán 25 évesen lett a BKK vezérigazgatója. 2022-ben, az ötödik Orbán-kormány alatt az államtitkárságig jutott, majd Lázár János érkezése után a fővárosban folytatta.

stories.title: A mező közepére építettek egy elektromos töltőállomást Romániában
stories.summary: A Máramaros megyei községben ráadásul már van egy töltőpont, és azt autóval meg is lehet közelíteni.

stories.title: Évek óta először nem voltak oroszok a teremben, mondta Tusk az Orbán nélküli EU-csúcsról, erre ott termett Fico
stories.summary: A lengyel miniszterelnök szerint a magyar választás következménye nagyon világos jelzés, Magyar Péter győzelmét egy sorozat részének látta.

stories.title: Szalai Vivien, a TV2 volt hírigazgatója szinte mindent letörölt az Instagramjáról
stories.summary: És át is nevezte magát ott, már a férje vezetéknevét használja.

stories.title: Átlátható vezetőválasztást és bérrendezést szeretne a hazai múzeumi szféra fontos szakmai szervezete
stories.summary: Az ICOM Magyarország 19 pontos állásfoglalásának ajánlásai között a fenntartói kontroll és a szakmai irányítás szétválasztása, illetve Iparművészeti Múzeum rekonstrukciójának azonnali elindítása is szerepel.

stories.title: Newsletter: EU leaders weigh a NATO-style common defence clause
stories.summary: Also in this newsletter: EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan and new Russia sanctions.

stories.title: Ukraine says Russian oil to Hungary, Slovakia has restarted
stories.summary: Energy companies in Ukraine and Hungary have both said that oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Russia via Belarus have restarted. Hungary and Slovakia expect the first supplies to arrive by Thursday.

stories.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
stories.summary: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
stories.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
stories.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

stories.title: Revealed: UK oil refinery owner moved Russian loans to offshore subsidiary where sanctions did not apply
stories.summary: MPs call for investigation into Essar Energy, owner of Stanlow refinery, which shifted loans from ‘Putin’s piggy bank’ VTB to Mauritius Days after the first wave of Russian tanks surged over the border into Ukraine in March 2022, dockers at a port in northern England took a stand. Appalled by Vladimir Putin’s brutality, workers at Ellesmere Port in Cheshire vowed never to unload any Russian oil destined for the nearby Stanlow refinery, a major hub for UK fuel supplies. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU must stop being ‘distant observer’ in Middle East, Cyprus warns
stories.summary: NICOSIA — European countries need to step up strategically in the Middle East, strike deals with partners across the region and try to bring an end to the Iran war, Cyprus’ foreign minister said Friday. “It’s clear that the European Union could and should be doing more,” when it comes to the conflict, Constantinos Kombos told POLITICO in an interview on the sidelines of the European Council in Nicosia. “The EU should be more present and more active. And this is the

stories.title: How frustration at Cop stalemates inspires first global talks on phasing out fossil fuels
stories.summary: ‘Coalition of the willing’ gathers in Colombia to try to bypass petrostate blockages of Cop summits and chart fresh path The world’s first Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels conference, co-hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands, takes place in Santa Marta, Colombia, from 24 to 29 April. A “coalition of the willing” – including 54 countries and various subnational governments, civil society groups and academics – will try to chart a new path to powering the world with low-carbon energy. Continu

stories.title: Tokyo workers encouraged to wear shorts to cut energy costs and keep cool
stories.summary: Officials hope more casual attire for public servants will save electricity during Iran war as summer heat approaches Public servants working for the Tokyo metropolitan government are being encouraged to swap their suits for shorts this summer to combat sweltering heat and rising energy costs caused by the US-Israel war on Iran . Inspired by Japan’s Cool Biz energy-saving initiative , Tokyo officials hope the measure will cut dependence on air conditioning. Continue reading...

stories.title: Pentagon email floats punishing NATO allies over Iran war
stories.summary: Washington has been considering sanctioning NATO allies over their non-involvement in the Iran war, with Spain in the spotlight after refusing the US use of its bases and airspace, according to reports.

stories.title: Face à la crise iranienne, le forage pétrogazier tente les pays les plus verts
stories.summary: BRUXELLES – Des représentants de dizaines de pays à la pointe de la lutte mondiale pour l’écologie se réunissent en Colombie à partir de vendredi pour défendre des mesures visant à abandonner les énergies fossiles. Mais au même moment, nombre d’entre eux peinent à résister à l’appel du pétrole et du gaz hautement polluants qui dorment sous leurs pieds. Poussés par une instabilité géopolitique croissante, et en premier lieu la guerre en Iran, les gouvernements cherchent to

stories.title: Former NATO envoy warns of ‘very big mistakes’ in criticizing Trump on Iran
stories.summary: The former U.S. ambassador to NATO is urging British and European politicians not to alienate Donald Trump by criticizing his administration’s handling of the Iran war and its fallout. Kurt Volker, the U.S. envoy to NATO in 2008-2009 who then served as the U.S. special representative for Ukraine during the first Trump administration, warned that European figures who have condemned the U.S. president’s approach are “making some very big mistakes.” “You might think that this is a huge folly and go

stories.title: Trump claims US has total control over strait of Hormuz after Iran seizes two container ships
stories.summary: US president says Tehran hobbled by infighting as Pentagon reportedly briefs mine clearance may take six months Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has again said that the US has “total control over the strait of Hormuz”, adding that Iran’s leadership was so hobbled by infighting that it was unclear who was in charge. But the US president’s claim seemed questionable in the face of the seizure of two container ships by Iranian commandos and a US report warning it could take six months

stories.title: US forces board vessel in Indian Ocean – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. For the latest Middle East news, see our full report here The Pentagon abruptly announced that the secretary of the US navy, John Phelan, would be leaving his job yesterday. No reason was given for the unexpected departure of the navy’s top civilian official, who had addressed a large crowd of sailors and industry professionals at the navy’s annual conference in Washington just a day before the announcement. People familiar with the dynamics at the Pentagon told the Guar

stories.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
stories.summary: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the

stories.title: Largest-ever ban on toxic chemicals in EU hit by ‘extremely frustrating’ delays
stories.summary: Green groups say European Commission is ‘chief roadblock’ to its own plans, as report finds poor progress four years on Harmful compounds in children’s nappies and toxic “forever chemicals” in everyday products are among 14 hazardous substance groups hit by lengthy delays to EU pollution controls, according to report findings described by scientists as “extremely frustrating”. The European Commission sought to push broad categories of dangerous substances off the market with a “restrictions road

stories.title: Embassy encourages Australia-based Iranians to volunteer to help war effort
stories.summary: Message on Telegram account has sparked alarm among diaspora groups, with one calling for the embassy to be closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tehran’s embassy in Canberra is promoting a recruitment drive for a paramilitary campaign to defend Iran from US forces, with the Australian federal police now looking into the matter. As the war approaches the two-month mark, the embassy’s website and Telegram channel this week posted a message that instructed people on h

stories.title: PM speaks to Vietnamese president on supply chains – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Allegra Spender says 25% gas export tax would help fix ‘faulty’ system Independent MP Allegra Spender said a 25% tax on gas exports would help rectify what she sees as “faulty” taxation arrangements that have seen an Australian resource sent overseas with minimal benefit to the country. The gas industry is a very profitable industry and pays income tax. And every company in Australia, frankly, should pay income

stories.title: Syria arrests suspected leader of Tadamon massacre
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef is one of most-wanted fugitives in relation to slaughter of estimated 288 civilians under Assad A Syrian former regime official suspected of leading a notorious civilian massacre revealed by the Guardian – and who became one of the country’s most-wanted fugitives after the fall of Bashar al-Assad – has been arrested by security forces, Syria’s interior ministry announced. Amjad Youssef was captured in the countryside about 30 miles (50km) outside the city of Hama and had “been take

stories.title: After Italian law change, Americans hope supreme court ruling will reopen door to citizenship
stories.summary: Sabrina Crawford among those refused citizenship because of new law stopping access via distant ancestry In 2025, after a long and arduous journey in her attempts to gain Italian citizenship, including a pivotal genealogical research trip to a village in Calabria, US-born Sabrina Crawford was hoping to fulfil her lifelong dream of building a life in Italy as she edged towards the final hurdle of the bureaucratic process. But her plans were scuppered when Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government ena

stories.title: Venice Biennale jury ‘will not award artists from countries facing war crimes charges’
stories.summary: Statement, apparently aimed at Russia and Israel, makes clear it is committed to the defence of human rights The jury of the Venice Biennale has said it would not give awards to artists from countries whose leaders are facing charges of crimes against humanity, in a statement apparently aimed at Russia and Israel. The five-member jury of the art exhibition said it was committed to “the defence of human rights”, in continuity with the vision established by Koyo Kouoh, the Swiss-Cameroonian curato

stories.title: EU leaders agree blueprint for mutual assistance pact amid Trump’s criticism of ‘very disappointing Nato’
stories.summary: Members will plan how to come to each other’s aid in the event of an attack as Nato faces worst crisis in its history Brussels officials will draw up a plan on how to use the EU’s little-known mutual assistance pact in the event of a foreign attack, as Donald Trump’s criticism of Nato intensifies. EU leaders have agreed that the European Commission “will prepare a blueprint” on how the bloc will respond if the mutual assistance clause is triggered, according to Nikos Christodoulides, the preside

stories.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
stories.summary: Internal email proposes US should reassess support for UK claim to islands because of lack of support for Iran war UK politics live – latest updates The UK’s position on the Falklands is resolute and unchanging, Downing Street has insisted, after a leaked Pentagon internal email proposed the US should reassess its support for Britain’s claim to the islands because of a lack of support over Iran. Keir Starmer’s spokesperson did not push back against the likely veracity of the email but insisted t

stories.title: Downing Street says sovereignty of Falkland Islands ‘rests with the UK’ after leaked Pentagon report – UK politics live
stories.summary: Report suggests the US could review its position on the British claim to the territory after lack of support in Iran war Downing Street has hit back at reports suggesting the US could reconsider its position over the UK’s claim to the Falkland Islands because the UK did not do enough to assist the American bombing of Iran was leaked. The prime minister’s official spokesperson said: “The UK position is clear and isn’t going to change … It’s a longstanding one. It’s an unchanged one, and it will r

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Hopes rise for renewed US-Iran talks as Tehran’s foreign minister reported to be heading to Pakistan
stories.summary: Iranian state media says Abbas Araqchi due to begin a trip today that includes visiting Islamabad, Muscat and Moscow The EU’s foreign chief has said that talks with Iran should include nuclear experts otherwise “we will end up with a more dangerous Iran.” Speaking on Friday ahead of an informal summit of EU leaders in Cyprus, EU’s foreign chief Kaja Kallas said: “If the talks are only about the nuclear and there are no nuclear experts around the table, then we will end up with an agreement that

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Trump bristles at Prince Harry’s passionate plea for Ukraine
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex says US must ‘honour obligations’ as it persuaded Ukraine to give up nuclear weapons; US president retorts that Harry doesn’t speak for Britain. What we know on day 1,521 Donald Trump has said the Duke of Sussex “is not speaking for the UK” after Prince Harry told the US to honour its obligations in the Ukrainian conflict . “I think I am speaking for the UK more than Prince Harry … But I appreciate his advice very much,” said Trump, responding to the duke’s lengthy, impassioned sp

stories.title: US soldier involved in Maduro raid charged over alleged bets on capture
stories.summary: Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who allegedly made more than $400,000 on Polymarket, could face up to 60 years in prison A US soldier who played a role in the January capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro is now in custody after allegedly cashing in over $400,000 on wagers about the politician’s removal from office, federal authorities announced on Thursday. Prosecutors say beginning in early December the soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was involved in planning for the military operation to capture

stories.title: US soldier allegedly made $400k on Polymarket betting on Maduro raid
stories.summary: Prosecutors say the soldier used classified information of the Maduro operation to place winning bets on Polymarket. The case comes amid other incidents of prediction market bets tied to US government actions.

stories.title: Thai opposition figures face trial over attempt to change royal defamation law
stories.summary: Forty-four lawmakers could be banned from office for seeking to change Thailand's strict lese-majeste law, which criminalizes criticism of the monarchy. Critics say it has been used to silence opponents.

stories.title: German economy growth forecasts halved
stories.summary: Germany's economy continues to falter under the shock of soaring energy prices. In the wake of the Iran war, the German economy minister has had to cut her own forecast by half.

stories.title: Germany news: Lawmakers vote to lower fuel tax amid high gas prices
stories.summary: People in Germany could see gas prices fall next week after lawmakers approved a bill to lower the fuel tax by €0.17 per liter. Follow DW for these stories and more.

stories.title: Spain responds to reported US plans to punish NATO allies
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly proposes a series of options to punish NATO allies for failing to provide support in the Iran war. Suspending Spain from the alliance was just one possibility it considered.

stories.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.title: France drops climate from G7 environment talks to appease Trump administration
stories.summary: PARIS — France removed climate change from the agenda of this week’s G7 talks to prevent a rift with the United States, according to a French government official. Environment ministers from the G7 nations are meeting in Paris Thursday and Friday to discuss issues ranging from biodiversity to water resources. But the French hosts deliberately avoided putting global warming on the agenda to appease the U.S. administration of President Donald Trump, who has dismissed climate change as a

stories.title: UK quietly increases AI emissions forecast 100-fold
stories.summary: LONDON — A senior Labour MP has joined campaigners in raising fresh concerns over the environmental impacts of AI, after the U.K. government quietly admitted carbon emissions from data centers could be 100 times greater than previously thought. Revised analysis accompanying a government “Compute Roadmap,” published on Thursday, shows U.K. emissions of greenhouse gas from AI could reach 123 million metric tons of CO2 (MtCO2) — the equivalent of 3.4 percent of the U.K.’s emissions between 2025 and

stories.title: Putin, Netanyahu get their countries barred from Venice Biennale top prizes
stories.summary: Russia and Israel have been barred from winning the Venice Biennale’s top prizes after the exhibition’s jury moved to exclude countries whose leaders have been indicted by the International Criminal Court. In a statement released Thursday, the jury said it decided to rule out awarding the Biennale’s Golden and Silver Lions, handed out at the exhibition’s closing in September, to national pavilions belonging to countries whose heads of state are facing ICC charges. The unprecedented decisio

stories.title: Meloni slams €800M EU building renovation works
stories.summary: NICOSIA — Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Friday criticized plans to renovate one of the EU’s main buildings in Brussels. Meloni said Brussels should set a good example when it comes to public spending, especially in the middle of tense negotiations over the bloc’s long-term budget. “We also need to send a clear message regarding administrative expenses,” Meloni told reporters on the sidelines of an informal EU summit in Nicosia. “We cannot propose renovati

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to attend first Anzac Day service since war crime charges
stories.summary: Former soldier says he will ‘pay my respects’ at commemoration in Queensland where he has been living since being released on bail Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ben Roberts-Smith will attend an Anzac Day service in Queensland on Saturday morning, describing the day as “sacred” to him, the first commemoration since he was criminally charged. Roberts-Smith, the recipient of the Victoria Cross and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of t

stories.title: Ice block stalls hundreds of Everest climbers at base camp
stories.summary: Officials assessing route after serac between base camp and camp one deemed unstable and too risky for climbers A large ice block on the route just above the Mount Everest base camp has forced hundreds of climbers and local guides to delay their attempt to scale the world’s highest peak. The serac between base camp and camp one was unstable and risky for climbers, said Himal Gautam of Nepal’s department of mountaineering on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Feeling gloomy about the economy? The ‘vibecession’ has arrived in Australia – but experts are less worried
stories.summary: A poll shows most Australians think the country is either in a recession or will be soon. Economists have a different view Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian households were already on edge before the bombs started falling in Iran. The cost of living was high and inflation was accelerating again, forcing the Reserve Bank to start ratcheting up interest rates. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump says he will ‘probably put a big tariff on the UK’ if it doesn’t drop digital services tax
stories.summary: President accuses Britain of trying to ‘make an easy buck’ from American tech firms, weeks after warning UK–US trade deal can be changed Business live – latest updates Donald Trump has threatened to impose “a big tariff” on the UK if it does not drop its digital services tax on US social media firms. The digital services tax, introduced in 2020, imposes a 2% levy on the revenues of several big US tech companies. Continue reading...

stories.title: Soundtrack of the sea: divers use underwater speakers to help dying coral reefs
stories.summary: Divers are installing waterproof speakers in the ocean to help pull a coral reef near Jamaica back from the brink The northern coast of Jamaica once served as the backdrop for scenes in the James Bond thriller No Time to Die. But today, beneath those same turquoise waves, a real-life mission is unfolding: the race to pull a dying coral reef back from the brink. However, the tools a team of divers are carrying to the seafloor are not what you would expect to find in a marine biologist’s kit. They

stories.title: Ukrainian soldiers left emaciated on frontline from lack of food and water
stories.summary: Top commander fired after wife of one malnourished soldier posted shocking images on social media Ukraine’s defence ministry has fired a top commander after photos emerged of a group of emaciated soldiers who have been left on the frontline for months without proper food and water. The scandal erupted after the wife of one of the soldiers, Anastasiia Silchuk, posted the images on social media. The four men appeared to be pale and visibly malnourished, with prominent ribcages and thin arms. Conti

stories.title: Retail sales rise in Britain after Iran war prompted ‘panic at the pumps’
stories.summary: ONS says sales rose by 0.7% in March, spurred by motorists filling their tanks and sunny weather helping retailers Business live – latest updates Motorists stocking up on fuel helped to push up retail sales in Great Britain last month as the Iran war prompted “panic at the pumps” amid rapid rises in petrol and diesel prices. The Office for National Statistics (ONS) said that the volume of retail sales rose by 0.7% last month, well above analysts’ forecasts of just 0.1%. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain should seek to rejoin EU, says civil servant who led Brexit department
stories.summary: Philip Rycroft says promises on issues from economics to immigration have not lived up to expectations Britain should start talking about rejoining the EU, according to a former senior civil servant who ran the Brexit department. Philip Rycroft, who was permanent secretary of the Department for Exiting the EU, said the “argument was there to be won” about going back into Europe, adding that a “clear-headed appraisal of what is in the country’s best interests” was needed. However, he said rejoini

stories.title: Microsoft and Meta cut thousands of staff as they bet big on AI | First Thing
stories.summary: Meta to lay off 10% of its staff and Microsoft to offer retirement to 7% of US workforce. Plus, Iron Maiden at 50 Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. Meta and Microsoft are cutting thousands of employees as they bet big on AI and executives claim that the technology is meeting productivity needs. What have they said about AI? Mark Zuckerberg said in January that AI was making some hiring unnecessary. Mustafa Suleyman, Microsoft’s AI chief, said in February tha

stories.title: Trump psychedelics order largely symbolic, analysts say
stories.summary: Executive order to speed access to psychedelic treatments likely to have limited legal impact despite high-profile push The Trump administration issued an executive order earlier this month to accelerate access to psychedelic medication for people with “serious mental illnesses”, but experts say the order is more likely to make a difference symbolically than legally. “Policymakers and the medical field have long struggled to address the burden of suicide and serious mental illness rates in Ameri

stories.title: Man, 32, jailed for life for rape and assault of Sikh woman in Walsall
stories.summary: John Ashby to serve minimum of 14 years after admitting rape, robbery, intentional strangulation and religiously aggravated assault A man has been jailed for life with a minimum term of 14 years for the religiously aggravated rape and assault of a Sikh woman. John Ashby, 32, had admitted charges of rape, robbery, intentional strangulation and religiously aggravated assault on Tuesday. Ashby asked to see his barrister and changed his pleas around an hour after being sworn at and told to “sort you

stories.title: Young people may not recognise they have been victims of stalking, says CPS
stories.summary: Crown Prosecution Service launches action plan to help potential victims and perpetrators understand behaviours Young people may not even realise they have been victims of stalking, the Crown Prosecution Service has said as it launched a scheme to tackle a rise in offending. The number of stalking offences charged by the CPS in England and Wales reached a record high last year of 7,168. More than 80% of these were flagged as relating to domestic abuse, meaning most victims were stalked or harass

stories.title: Take action over officials in Kyrgyzstan ‘helping Russia evade sanctions’, MPs and peers say
stories.summary: British foreign secretary told to impose new measures as ruble-pegged cryptocurrency A7A5 is supported in country More than 20 MPs and peers have called on the foreign secretary to take action against institutions and individuals in Kyrgyzstan allegedly facilitating large-scale Russian sanctions evasion. They urged the UK to levy personal sanctions against three top Kyrgyz officials for their alleged role in facilitating Russian sanctions evasion more broadly, and more specifically for allowing

stories.title: Meta, Microsoft purge jobs amid AI build-up
stories.summary: Facebook's parent company said it would slash 10% of its workforce, while Microsoft is offering an early retirement scheme. The cuts come as both tech giants make massive investments in AI.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon to extend ceasefire as US-Iran talks stall
stories.summary: The announcement came after a rare meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials at the White House. Meanwhile, the Strait of Hormuz is still closed amid stalled peace talks between the US and Iran. Follow DW.

stories.title: China's DeepSeek launches preview of new AI model
stories.summary: DeepSeek, the startup behind a low-cost chatbot that sent shockwaves through the AI community last year, has released its highly anticipated V4 model.

stories.title: Amid fuel crisis, a bold move to leave oil and gas behind
stories.summary: At an unprecedented meeting in Colombia, delegates from more than 50 countries want to chart a practical path to wean the world off fossil fuels. Can they succeed where the UN climate talks have failed?

stories.title: India news: Record turnout in Bengal, Tamil Nadu polls
stories.summary: West Bengal and Tamil Nadu recorded the highest-ever voter turnout in state polls after controversial voter roll revision. DW has more.

stories.title: Nepal: Mount Everest ice block obstructs large group of spring climbers
stories.summary: A chunk of ice suspended above the Khumbu Icefall near Base Camp on Mount Everest means it's not safe either for climbers or the specialist "icefall doctors" who typically clear away smaller obstructions.

stories.title: Iran talks should include nuclear experts: EU top diplomat
stories.summary: Kaja Kallas said failing to bring nuclear experts to the table in talks with Tehran could result in "a more dangerous ⁠Iran." EU leaders meeting in Cyprus are discussing the Middle East conflict, among other topics.

stories.title: Natural molecule could provide Ozempic, Wegovy alternative
stories.summary: Discovered using AI, a naturally occurring molecule appears to act directly in the brain's appetite center. Scientists hope it could refine treatments for obesity, offering fewer side effects for some patients.

stories.title: Indians forced to return home as Iran war rocks Gulf economy
stories.summary: Thousands of Indian workers were forced to leave their jobs in the Gulf nations behind and return home due to the Iran war. Their future remains unclear, and now India itself is feeling the economic impact.

stories.title: ICC says Philippines' Duterte can stand trial over war on drugs
stories.summary: The ICC has said there is enough evidence linking former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte to crimes against humanity over his role in orchestrating a deadly anti-drugs crackdown.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by three weeks, Trump says
stories.summary: Trump said Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, which was set to expire on Monday, by three weeks. Meanwhile, the US and Iran persist in its tense standoff in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran war sparks push to transform Syria into global energy corridor
stories.summary: Washington is reportedly exploring a plan to turn Syria into a key energy corridor, linking their pipelines to Turkey and Europe.

stories.title: Epstein housed abuse victims in London flats, BBC reveals
stories.summary: The revelations intensify concerns about repeated police decisions not to investigate 2015 trafficking claims.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Kremlin's tightening grip on internet fuels Russian discontent
stories.summary: Officials say the restrictions are for public safety, but businesses and the public are feeling the impact.

stories.title: Over 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN Women report estimates
stories.summary: From becoming the head of a household and facing challenges linked to housing and land rights, to restricted access to essential healthcare and reproductive services, how are women disproportionately affected by war?

stories.title: Armenia marks 111th anniversary of 1915 massacres in Yerevan
stories.summary: Armenia marked the 111th anniversary of the 1915 massacres on 23 and 24 April 2026 with marches and ceremonies in Yerevan, drawing thousands of participants.

stories.title: 20,000 teddy bears in Washington highlight abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: A sea of 20,000 teddy bears on the US National Mall highlights Ukrainian children abducted since 2021, urging action as the war’s toll on families grows.

stories.title: Aboriginal children's book pulled over illustrator's Bondi attack comments
stories.summary: Several writers have cut ties with the University of Queensland Press for cancelling the book.

stories.title: Meta to cut one in 10 jobs after spending billions on AI
stories.summary: The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

stories.title: South Korea police arrest man for posting AI photo of runaway wolf
stories.summary: The widely circulated image had prompted authorities to move their search operation.

stories.title: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses
stories.summary: The woman got stuck waist-deep in a pit latrine in the Australian outback after it gave way.

stories.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
stories.summary: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.

stories.title: In pictures: North India braces for heatwaves as temperatures cross 40C
stories.summary: India's weather department has issued heatwave warnings in parts of northern India.

stories.title: 'A dangerous standoff' as Strait of Hormuz blockade continues
stories.summary: The US and Iran's rival blockades of the Strait of Hormuz have become a "test of wills", says Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: Judge dismisses Smokey Robinson's defamation claim against rape accusers
stories.summary: The veteran singer had made a counterclaim after four housekeepers sued him for alleged sexual assault.

stories.title: UK biobank data incident caused by 'a few bad apples', boss says

stories.summary: Dionne Marie Hanna, 85, is accused of duping more than two dozen people - including her own son.

stories.summary: Former Iranian diplomat Kourosh Ahmadi has warned that Tehran and Washington have found themselves stuck in their individual and contrasting narratives, risking nuclear talks as tensions over blockades and enrichment grow.

stories.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.title: EU Parliament to spend €22M revamping visitor center
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Parliament will on Monday green-light a €22.7 million overhaul of its visitor center, according to internal documents seen by POLITICO. The Parlamentarium center, which explains to visitors how the Parliament works and its history, opened in 2011 and is located right next to the Brussels HQ. The plan is to make the center more visible from Place du Luxembourg and fit it with the latest technology. The renovation will cost more than the €20 million it cost to build the Par

stories.title: Kazakhstan confirms suspension of its oil transit to Germany via Russia
stories.summary: Kazakhstan’s energy minister announced Wednesday there would be no transit of its oil to Germany through Russia throughout May due to Russian technical constraints, assuming it is “related to recent strikes on Russian infrastructure".

stories.title: UN rights chief alarmed by number of drugs-related executions in Singapore
stories.summary: Officials in Singapore maintain that the death penalty has made it one of the world's safest cities and a government survey in 2023 showed strong public support for capital punishment for serious crimes.

stories.title: Prince Harry tells Putin 'there is still a moment to stop this war', at Kyiv conference
stories.summary: Duke of Sussex was speaking at the Kyiv Security Forum during a surprise visit to show support for Ukraine after four years of war with Russia. The prince added that it was also 'a moment for America to show that it can honour its international treaty obligations'. His last visit to the country was in September 2025, when he discussed support for war veterans Continue reading...

stories.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
stories.summary: Prosecutors say 43 people indicted on charges including murder, kidnapping, extortion and drug trafficking More than two dozen members and associates of the Mexican mafia were arrested during an early morning crackdown in southern California, federal authorities said on Thursday. The FBI and other federal and local agencies executed search and arrest warrants at locations mostly in Orange county, south of Los Angeles, according to the US attorney’s office. Continue reading...

stories.title: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a ‘hellhole’
stories.summary: Foreign ministry says remarks of conservative podcast host Michael Savage that were shared by US president were ‘obviously uninformed, inappropriate and in poor taste’ India on Thursday criticised as inappropriate a post by Donald Trump in which he shared comments that called the South Asian country a “hellhole”. The inflammatory post on Truth Social comes ahead of a planned visit next month to India by the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is seeking to ease recent tensions between the no

stories.title: Germany unveils first-ever military strategy for Bundeswehr
stories.summary: For the first time in its history, the Bundeswehr has adopted a military strategy to prepare Germany for future threats. Large sections of the document are classified.

stories.title: UK: Health data volunteered by 500,000 people to Biobank charity listed for sale on Alibaba in China
stories.summary: The British government is investigating how health data volunteered to a charity for research purposes wound up for sale via three vendors on the Alibaba e-commerce site in China. One listing had 500,000 people's data.

stories.title: Samsung workers rally, call for larger share of AI profits
stories.summary: Unions at Samsung are threatening a weekslong strike if demands are not met. A production halt at one of the world's largest memory chipmakers could disrupt global chip supplies.

stories.title: Is Ethiopia bracing for war?
stories.summary: Analysts say the war in Iran has temporarily delayed an escalation in smoldering tensions between Ethiopia and Eritrea. But renewed armed conflict cannot be ruled out.

stories.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
stories.summary: With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests. DW takes a look at the main sticking points and explains some key issues.

stories.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Questions remain about the scope of the operation and whether it can bring lasting stability.

stories.title: Warner Bros shareholders greenlight sale to Paramount
stories.summary: The deal would combine two major Hollywood studios and bring CBS and CNN under one roof, tightening an already concentrated media landscape, critics say.

stories.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
stories.summary: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

stories.title: US and Iran continue seizing ships in tense Hormuz standoff
stories.summary: The US and Iran continued seizing ships in the tense Hormuz standoff, with Tehran calling Washington's blockade a “ceasefire violation” and claiming it has been collecting transit fees for passage.

stories.summary: The approval came as Donald Trump is to attend a dinner with billionaire Paramount backers the Ellisons.

stories.summary: Ráadásul újabb allergének virágzása csap le az arra érzékenyekre.

stories.summary: Az Országgyűlés alakuló ülésén teszik vissza a zászlót.

stories.summary: Már csőben van a megállapodás, amit a Yettelt birtokló céggel jelentettek be, de más bizniszek is kinézhetnek a NER egyik zászlóshajójának.

stories.summary: A nyomozók figyelmének középpontjába most a nő nagykorú lánya került, aki túlélte a végzetes karácsonyi vacsorát, mert a barátaival ment pizzázni.

stories.summary: Nem bünteti többé aránytalanul a rendelet azokat, akik közös megegyezéssel, maguktól hagyják ott a munkahelyüket.

stories.summary: A Direkt36 írta meg egy idős, de életerős nő történetét, aki erős fájdalmakkal került a Szent Imre Kórházba, de fia szerint nem kapott állapotának megfelelő ellátást. Órákkal később az otthonában meghalt szívinfarktusban.

stories.title: Leállította a kormányhivatal Mészáros Lőrinc cégének környezetpusztító tevékenységét a Vértesben

stories.title: Vitézy Dávid lesz a közlekedési és beruházási miniszter

stories.title: Új építésű ingatlan vásárlásának buktatói és megoldási lehetőségei

stories.title: Kallas: Most felül kellene vizsgálnunk a szankciók egy részét blokkoló határvonalakat

stories.title: Óriásit kaszált egy katona, aki tudott a venezuelai elnök elleni titkos akcióról, és Maduro bukására fogadott
stories.summary: Az amerikai katonát most letartóztatták, többek között bennfentes kereskedelemmel vádolják.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt: Be fogjuk vezetni a mentők kiérkezési idejének rendszeres, nyilvános közzétételét
stories.summary: A leendő egészségügyi miniszter szerint a rendszer csak így lesz átlátható, és így lehet javítani rajta.

stories.summary: Állkapocselemzésekből következtetnek arra, hogy akár húszméteresek is lehettek az uszonyos polipok, amelyek túlnőtték a gerinces vetélytársakat is.

stories.title: A Fidesz–KDNP-frakció többsége nemmel szavazott, most sem oszlatták fel a győri közgyűlést
stories.summary: Pintér Bence polgármester megint hiába próbálkozott, a közgyűlés stabil többségét adó Fidesz-frakció nem akarja bolygatni az erőviszonyokat.

stories.title: Börtönt kér az ügyészség a Morrison's 2-ben két ütéssel megölt 25 éves férfi ügyében
stories.summary: Az ügyészség kizárná a feltételes szabadon bocsátás lehetőségét.

stories.title: Az Alkotmánybíróság ülésén szólították fel lemondásra Polt Pétert, aki részletesen válaszolt is a vádakra
stories.summary: Szabó Marcel alkotmánybíró szerint a Polt múltjával terhelt intézményt már csak az elnök távozása mentheti meg.

stories.title: Nem emelnek vádat Ruszin-Szendi ellen a Mandiner munkatársának meglökése miatt
stories.summary: Garázdaság miatt javasolt volna vádemelést a rendőrség a Tisza politikusa ellen.

stories.title: „Ámokfutó”, „áruló” – Az éveken át tartó Lázár–Vitézy-beef legerősebb üzenetei
stories.summary: A két politikus közt kifejezetten feszült volt a viszony, összeszedtük, milyen válogatott módon szóltak be egymásnak az elmúlt években.

stories.title: A D-vitamin segíthet megelőzni a cukorbetegséget bizonyos genetikai variációknál
stories.summary: Egy amerikai kutatás prediabéteszes felnőtteknél vizsgálta a D-vitamin hatását. Nem mindenkinek segít, de sokaknál késleltetheti a súlyos szövődmények kialakulását.

stories.title: Lendíthet az ingatlanpiacon a Tisza győzelme, de egyelőre óvatosak a vevők
stories.summary: Sok vevő kivárt a választásokig, és bár maradtak bizonytalansági tényezők, jövőre már az uniós pénzek gazdaságélénkítő hatásai és az alacsonyabb kamatok is növelhetik az ingatlanpiaci forgalmat.

stories.title: A haverjaikat jelölték a legfontosabb brit művészeti díjra a zsűritagok a Guardian szerint
stories.summary: A Turner-díj jelöltjeinek listája korábban is heves vitákat váltott ki. Az egykor az újítónak és felforgatónak számító kitüntetés azonban mostanra teljesen érdektelenné vált a kritikusai szerint.

stories.title: Törvénytelenül gyűjtött adatokat a honlapján Lázár János a NAIH szerint
stories.summary: A miniszter az új KRESZ véleményezésével kapcsolatban gyűjtött adatokat.

stories.title: Mindenre kiterjedő vizsgálatot lengetett be a honvédségnél Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz
stories.summary: A honvédelmi tárca háttérintézményeinek működését és gazdálkodását is átvilágítják majd.

stories.title: Mészáros Lőrinc hulladékkezelő cége szerint nem állapítottak meg környezetszennyezést a telephelyükön
stories.summary: Két köbméter olaj folyt el az oroszlányi telephelynél.

stories.title: A 6000 milliárdos védelmi hitelből valószínűleg már nem jut a NER-nek
stories.summary: Újabb Fidesz-győzelem esetén a SAFE-hitel meghatározó része kormányközeli szereplőknél landolhatott volna, az új kormány azonban a hírek szerint átdolgozza a még jóvá nem hagyott kérelmet.

stories.title: A Tanítanék Mozgalom örömmel fogadta, hogy Lannert Judit lesz a Tisza-kormány oktatási minisztere
stories.summary: A civil szervezet szerint Lannert Judit nyitott, szakmailag felkészült, széles látókörű gondolkodó.

stories.title: The international right has CPAC. Has the left finally found its answer? | Owen Jones
stories.summary: Spain’s PM, Pedro Sánchez, hosted the inaugural meeting of the Global Progressive Mobilisation. Keir Starmer and other social democrats were notable by their absence Can progressives push back the rising tide of authoritarianism? Thousands of people gathered in Barcelona this weekend in search of an answer. The occasion was the inaugural Global Progressive Mobilisation – an ambitious initiative backed by the Spanish prime minister, Pedro Sánchez – which drew an impressive cast: Brazil’s Lula, Me

stories.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
stories.summary: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder i

stories.title: Opinion: The courage to speak
stories.summary: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, my experience as a rabbi showed that even small moments of dialogue can begin to rebuild fragile bridges between communities.

stories.title: France holds national tribute for soldier killed in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Minister for the Armed Forces, Catherine Vautrin, the Chief of Staff of the Army and General Pierre Schill preside over a national tribute ceremony in Montauban to honour the memory of Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio.

stories.title: British woman died in Ghana trying to recoup money from scammers, inquest told
stories.summary: Janet Fordham died in crash after travelling to see man who claimed he would help to recover money from earlier scams A British woman who was scammed out of up to £1m in a string of so-called romance frauds died in a road crash after travelling to west Africa to try to recoup some of her lost fortune, an inquest in Devon has heard. Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inques

stories.title: Pilot’s selfie led to mid-air collision in F-15K fighter jet, says South Korea’s air force
stories.summary: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilot

stories.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
stories.summary: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Climate protection must not hold economy back
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the public would not accept climate policies that lead to deindustrialization, warning that they would hinder progress and innovation.

stories.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
stories.summary: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

stories.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
stories.summary: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

stories.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
stories.summary: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

stories.title: A booklover's dream holiday in Scotland
stories.summary: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

stories.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
stories.summary: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

stories.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
stories.summary: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.

stories.title: Madrid hosts Experience Macao Roadshow showcasing culture, food and tourism
stories.summary: The Experience Macao Roadshow in Madrid offered visitors interactive activities, cultural performances and gastronomy highlighting the Asian territory’s tourism appeal.

stories.title: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire went into effect in Lebanon on Friday, but there have been several Israeli strikes and Hezbollah claimed its first attack on Tuesday.

stories.title: Second French peacekeeper dies after ambush blamed on Hezbollah, Macron says
stories.summary: Established in 1978, UNIFIL has more than 10,000 peacekeepers from 50 countries who patrol the Blue Line, the UN-drawn border between Lebanon and Israel.

stories.title: Beijing artist marks World Earth Day with peace installation
stories.summary: Chinese artist Kong Ning marks World Earth Day in Beijing with a peace-themed installation, using wearable art to highlight environmental concerns.

stories.title: Donald Trump says the US is in no rush to end war but 'clock is ticking' for Iran
stories.summary: Earlier in the day, Trump said he had ordered the US Navy to "shoot and kill" any boats found laying sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz.
